title: Schema Compatibility Structure
description: Structural documentation for the different types of schema compatibility and their implications for API and event streaming evolution.

backwardCompatible.label: Backward Compatible
backwardCompatible.definition: New schema can read data written with the old schema. Consumers using the new schema can process messages produced with the old schema.
backwardCompatible.useCase: Producer updates happen before consumer updates

forwardCompatible.label: Forward Compatible
forwardCompatible.definition: Old schema can read data written with the new schema. Consumers using the old schema can process messages produced with the new schema.
allowedChanges: Add required fields (old consumer ignores unknown fields)
forbiddenChanges: Add required fields to the reading schema
Remove fields that old consumers depend on
forwardCompatible.useCase: Consumer updates happen before producer updates

fullyCompatible.label: Fully Compatible
fullyCompatible.definition: Satisfies both backward AND forward compatibility. Any version can read data written by any other version.
forbiddenChanges: Any change that breaks backward or forward compatibility

breaking.label: Breaking Change
breaking.definition: A change that is neither backward nor forward compatible. Requires coordinated deployment of all producers and consumers.
examples: Add a required field without a default

addOnly.label: Add-Only Strategy
addOnly.description: Never remove or modify fields; only add new optional fields. The most conservative approach ensuring maximum compatibility.
addOnly.tradeoff: Schemas accumulate deprecated fields over time

versionedEndpoints.label: Versioned API Endpoints
versionedEndpoints.description: Create new API versions (/v2, /v3) for breaking changes. Maintain old versions until consumers migrate.
versionedEndpoints.tradeoff: Maintenance burden of supporting multiple versions simultaneously

fieldDeprecation.label: Field Deprecation Period
fieldDeprecation.description: Mark fields as deprecated and give consumers a migration window before removal.
fieldDeprecation.tradeoff: Requires consumer awareness and active migration

schemaRegistry.label: Schema Registry Enforcement
schemaRegistry.description: Use a schema registry to enforce compatibility rules automatically on every schema change before promotion.
schemaRegistry.tradeoff: Requires registry infrastructure and process discipline
